progress_summary: PRIOR FEATURE CLOSED: v1.6 shipped 2026-06-25 (PR #351) and v1.7 'Seams & Signals' shipped 2026-06-26 (PRs #356-359, site flip #359/#360) — launch phase of the v1.6 checkpoint is complete. CURRENT QUICK-FEATURE: blog content program (branch claude/vigilant-rhodes-6ebe72). Backfilled the 2026-06-25→07-01 publishing gap with 7 posts grounded in real repo history (v1.6 + v1.7 release narratives, cost-report pair incl. the public 13× correction #361→#364, telemetry stance, wire-1.1 protocol playbook, '27 skills' sequel), published 07-02 agent-discovery post, staged 07-03 deploy-pipeline post as draft:true, wrote docs/blog-content-calendar.md (M/W/F slate 07-03→08-01, 13 slots + spares + runbook), and reconciled docs/blog-overhaul-plan.md (Wave 3 shipped). Evaluator pass green: astro build 67 pages, astro check 0 errors, vitest 448/448, OG gen 19 blog cards, draft excluded from paths/RSS/OG, zero dead internal links, all frontmatter within schema caps, hero = 13x postmortem (featured).

progress_table.label: Discovery (from context: blog infra + release history recon)
Content plan (9 posts + 30-day calendar, §8 QA rubric)
Build — 9 posts, calendar doc, plan reconcile

key_decisions: Backdated posts are grounded in real repo events only (git log + skills/CHANGELOG.md + PR numbers) — no invented history. The 13x cost-report correction (#361→#364, $937.41 → $71.73) is told as a two-post arc: original annotated with a correction banner (house precedent: annotate, never rewrite), follow-up postmortem is the new featured hero.

Future-dated posts use draft:true as the only embargo — the site is static with no date filtering, so a future-dated published post would render immediately on deploy. Documented in the calendar runbook.
Calendar lives at docs/blog-content-calendar.md, NOT under site/src/blog/ (the content-collection glob would ingest it and fail the zod schema).

Calendar extends blog-overhaul-plan.md instead of rivaling it: §8 QA checklist retained as the per-post gate, S6 '~2/mo, no quota' principle preserved via an explicit slip-don't-fill rule; July runs M/W/F deliberately (launch-window backlog).

Series ledger: 'Dogfooding opchain' grew to 5 parts (cost pair joined); July opens 'Seams & Signals in practice' (4 parts: modularize playbook, golden-fixture anatomy, fleet playbook, signal playbook).
Hero rotation relies on newest-featured-wins in blog/index.astro — old featured flags don't need retiring.
Voice: house style per §3 (confident, technical, slightly irreverent, shows the seams) with humor dialed up on user request; every post keeps descriptions 120–160 chars and ≥2 internal links.
user_preferences: Picks options when given clear choices; comfortable with 'go with defaults'.
Prefers sharp scoping questions, then approves big plans in one pass.
Answers structured AskUserQuestion rounds quickly and decisively.
Manual deploy from a wrangler-logged-in laptop is the rule; CI is verification-only.
Blog: wants creative + funny + technologically exact; honesty-first house voice extends to jokes.

next_actions: Commit via /oc-git-sync (oc-bug-check gates) and open a PR from claude/vigilant-rhodes-6ebe72 to main; CI must be green (vitest + astro check + build + e2e + LHCI).
After merge: npm run deploy:staging from main, eyeball /blog (hero = 13x post, 19 posts, RSS), then npm run deploy — this publishes everything through the 07-02 post.
On 2026-07-03: flip draft:false on 2026-07-03-we-deleted-our-deploy-pipeline-on-purpose.md and deploy (the calendar runbook documents the flow).
Follow docs/blog-content-calendar.md M/W/F slate from Jul 6 (next up: 'Cut a live monolith without losing a byte'); slots slip rather than ship filler.
Around Jul 29: run /oc-release plan to fill the v1.8 release-post slot, or slip it.
